summary: Russian oil companies have suffered over one trillion rubles in losses from drone strikes on refineries, according to insurance estimates. On the Pokrovsk front, Ukrainian units destroyed enemy drones, vehicles, and electronic warfare systems, with UAV operators demonstrating high effectiveness. Ukrainian forces are deploying new drone technologies, including fiber-optic FPV drones immune to jamming and operating on non-standard frequencies, which are disrupting Russian logistics and forcing them into defensive scrambles. Russian forces are countering with systems like the "Yolka" anti-drone system and log-periodic antennas on "Molniyas" to improve range and resistance to jamming. Meanwhile, both sides are experiencing significant losses to artillery and armored vehicles from FPV drone strikes, with Ukrainian drones targeting Russian rear infrastructure and Russian forces adapting with new air defense and anti-drone tactics.
